# Essay Concerning Human Understanding

*[John Locke](../philosophers/locke.md) · 1689*

The founding work of classical empiricism, in which Locke refutes [innate ideas](../glossary/innate-ideas.md) and holds that the mind is originally a [blank slate](../glossary/tabula-rasa.md) filled only by experience. He also maps the bounds of the understanding and grounds **personal identity** in the continuity of consciousness.
